text: Welcome to the Texas Instruments First Quarter 2026 earnings conference call.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: I'm Mike Beckman, Head of Investor Relations, and I'm joined by our Chief Executive Officer,
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Havi Vellan, and our Chief Financial Officer, Rafael Lazardi.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: For any of you who missed the release, you can find it on our website at ti.com.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: This call is being broadcast live over the web and can be accessed through our website.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: In addition, today's call is being recorded and will be available via replay on our website.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: This call will include forward-looking statements that involve risks and uncertainties
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: that could cause TI's results to differ materially from management's current expectations.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: We encourage you to review the notice regarding forward-looking statements
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: contained in the earnings release published today, as well as TI's most recent SEC filings
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: for a more complete description.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Today, we'll provide the following updates.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: First, Havi will start with a quick overview of the quarter.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Next, he will provide insight into first quarter revenue results
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: with some details on what we're seeing with respect to our end markets.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Lastly, Rafael will cover the financial results, give an update on capital management,
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: as well as share the guidance for second quarter 2026.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: With that, let me turn it over to Havi.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Thanks, Mike.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Before I go into the results, I want to highlight that in the first quarter,
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: we announced an agreement for TI to acquire Silicon Labs.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: This transaction enhances our global leadership in embedded wireless connectivity,
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: expands TI's portfolio, and leverages TI's internally owned technology and manufacturing,
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: and rich-of-market channels.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: We expect a transaction to close in the first half of 2027, subject to necessary approvals.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Now, let me provide a quick overview of the first quarter.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Revenue was $4.8 billion, an increase of 9% sequentially,
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: and an increase of 19% year-over-year.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Analog and embedded both grew sequentially and year-on-year.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Analog revenue grew 22% year-on-year, and embedded processing grew 12%.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Our other segment declined 16% from the year-ago quarter.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Let me provide a few comments about the current market environment.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: In the first quarter, revenue came in above the top of the range,
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: as we saw continued acceleration in industrial and data center.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: The overall semiconductor market recovery is continuing,
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: and we remain well-positioned with inventory and capacity
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: that allows us to support our customers with competitive lead times through the cycle.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Now, I'll show some additional insights into first quarter revenue by end market.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: First, industrial increased more than 30% year-on-year,
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: and was up more than 20% sequentially, growing broadly across all sectors and regions.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Automotive increased mid-single-digits year-on-year, and was about flat sequentially.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Data center grew about 90% year-on-year, and grew more than 25% sequentially.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Personal electronics was flat year-on-year, and grew low single-digits sequentially.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: And lastly, communications equipment grew about 25% year-on-year,
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: and grew more than 30% sequentially.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: With that, let me turn it over to Rafael to review profitability and capital management.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Thanks, Avid, and good afternoon, everyone.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: As Avid mentioned, first quarter revenue was $4.8 billion.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Cross-profit in the quarter was $2.8 billion, or 58% of revenue.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Sequentially, gross profit margin increased 210 basis points.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Operating expenses in the quarter were $974 million, about as expected.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: On a trail-in-to-a-month basis, operating expenses were $3.9 billion, or 21% of revenue.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Operating profit was $1.8 billion in the quarter, or 37% of revenue, and was up 37% from the year-goer quarter.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Net income in the quarter was $1.5 billion, or $1.68 per share.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Earnings per share included a 5 cent benefit for items not in our original guidance,
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: primarily due to discrete tax benefits.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Let me now comment on our capital management results, starting with a cash generation.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Cash flow from operation was $1.5 billion in the quarter, and $7.8 billion on a trail-in-to-a-month basis.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Capital expenditures were $676 million in the quarter, and $4.1 billion over the last 12 months.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Free cash flow on a trail-in-to-a-month basis was $4.4 billion, up from $1.7 billion in the first quarter of 2025.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Trending up as growth returns and capex begins to moderate.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Free cash flow in the trail-in-to-a-month includes $965 million of CHIP's ACT incentives.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: This includes a $555 million payment received in the first quarter as part of our direct funding agreement,
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: related to the startup production at our newest 300mm wafer fab in Sherman, Texas.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: In the quarter, we pay $1.3 billion in dividends and repurchase $158 million of our stock.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: In total, we return $6 billion to our owners in the past 12 months.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Our balance sheet remains strong with $5.1 billion of cash and short-term investments at the end of the first quarter.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Total debt outstanding is $14 billion. We have weighted average coupon of 4%.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Inventory at the end of the quarter was $4.7 billion.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Down $109 million from the prior quarter and days were $209 down 13 days sequentially.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Turning to our outlook for the second quarter, we expect TI's revenue in the range of $5 billion to $5.4 billion,
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: and earnings per share to be in the range of $1.77 to $2.05.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: We expect our effective tax rate to be about 13% in the second quarter.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: In closing, we will stay focused in the areas that add value in the long term.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: We continue to invest in our competitive advantages, which are manufacturing and technology, a broad product portfolio,
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: reach of our channels and diverse and long-lived positions.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: We will continue to strengthen these advantages through disciplined capital allocation and by focusing on the best opportunities,
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: which we believe will enable us to continue to deliver free cash, super share growth over the long term.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: With that, let me turn it back to Mike.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Operator, you can now open the line for questions.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: In order to provide as many of you as possible an opportunity to ask your questions, please let me yourself to a single question.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: After our response, we'll provide you an opportunity for an additional follow-up. Operator?
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Thank you.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Well, now we conducted a question and answer session.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: If you would like to ask your question, please press star 1 on your telephone keypad.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: A confirmation tone will indicate your line is in the question queue.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: You may press star 2 to remove your question from the queue.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: For participants using speaker equipment, it may be necessary to pick up the handset before processing the star keys.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: One moment, please, while we pull for questions.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thank you.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Our first question is from Tim Arcary with UBS.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thanks a lot.
speaker: TIMOTHY ARCURI - UBS

text: I wonder if you can comment just on the behavior of customers.
speaker: TIMOTHY ARCURI - UBS

text: I know you're guiding up a little better than seasonal off of the number in March that was very strong.
speaker: TIMOTHY ARCURI - UBS

text: It sounds like it's mostly industrial, but can you comment on the rush orders?
speaker: TIMOTHY ARCURI - UBS

text: I know we're seeing signs of price increases and things like that.
speaker: TIMOTHY ARCURI - UBS

text: Is this impacting the customer's behavior? Thanks.
speaker: TIMOTHY ARCURI - UBS

text: Thanks, Tim. In general, I think Q1 was a continuation of what we saw in Q4, very similar behavior, meaning growth coming from too many areas led by industrial, as you mentioned.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Also supported by the data center market that we've seen a secular growth over there for the last couple of years.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: This was the eight-quarter of sequential growth, just off of a higher number.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: That also helps the overall growth of the company.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I would say that industrial signal was a little bit broader this time.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I would say all sectors, all geographies grew sequentially, and it continued to accelerate through the quarter.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: If you think about January, February, and then you always want to see how the exit from the lunar or the Chinese new year break is going to look like, but it continued in March.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Just a continuation.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I would say it's now five or six months of continued growth in industrial.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: We want to keep watching it, but I would say that's what guides our forecast into the second quarter.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Mike, anything to add on that?
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I think we just want to be mindful to just the overall macro backdrop and want to see how sustainable the growth is, and that was factored into the guide.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Tim, do you have a follow-up?
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I do.
speaker: TIMOTHY ARCURI - UBS

text: Mike, maybe you can comment on... I know typically you don't break the guidance down by segment, but just given how different it was in March, and given that we're hearing some choppiness in autos, particularly in China, I mean, I would think that most of this sequential growth will be in industrial,
speaker: TIMOTHY ARCURI - UBS

text: but can you give any comments for what is being thought of in the June guidance for those two? Thanks.
speaker: TIMOTHY ARCURI - UBS

text: Let me take that, Tim.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I think I can help you a little bit on the automotive side.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: But first, I think, as you said, we are not seeing a change from the previous quarter, so I expect growth to be led by industrial and data center.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I won't break it out between the two, but we see it's printing both.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Regarding automotive, you're right. The Q1 was always the same in Q1 in China.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: The overall quarter was flat sequentially, but China was down, the rest of the world was up.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I want to see automotive and see how it develops in Q2. It's too soon to call it.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I will remind us, though, that during the COVID cycle, even automotive was the last to join in, also the last to pick.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: So I'm not surprised by the behavior of this market.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I will say that secular growth in automotive continues for the foreseeable future, and that is my encouragement.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: We are seeing cars adding features. We are seeing more content added to vehicles across the powertrain, whether it's BV or ice or the hybrids.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Anything to add on that, Mike, in terms of the guide?
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: No, I think you characterize it well.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: And as you know, Auto has been steady at an elevated level for some time.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: It didn't really have that steep correction that we saw on the other end market.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: I think it's a view called it out. These markets have been in the past, have been transitioning out of phase.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: I don't think it's unrealistic to assume that could happen again. So, what to see how it plays out.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Yeah, I think it's important, and point that Mike said, Q1 was a flat quarter, but very close to peak levels, maybe a point or two below it speaks.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: So it's holding very nicely at a high level.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: All right, we'll move on to our next caller.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Thank you. Our next question is from Vivek Arya with Bank of America.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thanks for taking my question. We won this industrial growth up 30%. I think you said year on year.
speaker: VIVEK ARYA - BOFA SECURITIES

text: This is obviously well above the long-term trend line. Could you help us dissect which applications, which end markets are driving this?
speaker: VIVEK ARYA - BOFA SECURITIES

text: Is it still inventory replenishment? Is it pricing? Is it share gains?
speaker: VIVEK ARYA - BOFA SECURITIES

text: Just what kind of checks and balances do you have in place that this isn't any kind of double ordering or holding of your product?
speaker: VIVEK ARYA - BOFA SECURITIES

text: No, I don't see it. At least I don't have the evidence to show that Vivek. But remember, in Australia you said, yeah, for one quarter that's a lot of growth.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: But if you look at the long-term trend line, we are still below the trend line.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: So if I just did the math, in Q1, our industrial, we had a very good quarter in industrial growing at the rate that you've mentioned, but still 15% lower than the peak that was back in 2022.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: And as I say many times, there is a secular growth continuing in industrial. So we deserve a higher peak, right, four years later.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: So I think there is a lot of room to grow. The encouragement I would have on industrial this time is that I see it at a broader application.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: So all of them, not only the data center related, the energy infrastructure or power delivery, not only aerospace and defense.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I mean, all the geopolitical tensions in the market is establishing new peaks every quarter. I saw it across all sectors in industrial and also across all customers in terms of regions, but also the size of customers.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: It's the first quarter where we saw the broad market, as we call it, the tail starting to wake up again after a long hibernation period, I would call it.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: So I am encouraged about the fact that we are seeing growth over there, but I think there is, I mean, I would like to see a secular growth in industrial continuing and then higher peaks establishing in 2026 or later versus the 2022 peak.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: So in that sense, trend line, our suggesting we still have room to go. Hopefully that helps. You will follow the back.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Yes, thank you, Mike. So last year, we saw the overall analog industry do very well in the first half, and then there were some level of deceleration in the second half.
speaker: VIVEK ARYA - BOFA SECURITIES

text: I've realized every year is different. And I know you're not guiding to the second half, but from what you see today, what are the puts and takes as you look at the second half versus the first half?
speaker: VIVEK ARYA - BOFA SECURITIES

text: Is there anything that could be different just given by all the macro trends, memory, price inflation and whatnot?
speaker: VIVEK ARYA - BOFA SECURITIES

text: And as part of that, if Rassal could also help chime in with how you are managing fab loadings as you look towards the rest of the year. Thank you.
speaker: VIVEK ARYA - BOFA SECURITIES

text: Yeah, let me start in Rafael. We follow. So first, we make your spot on, right? We had a similar, let's say, strong beginning of the year, last year, maybe, maybe the year over year, and growth, the last year was a little lower.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: But it was still in the teens, and it looks like it was getting stronger, but it was whatever you want to call it, the head fake, a false start or whatever.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: We had a good year in analog, but it did not accelerate in the second half. It actually slowed down a little bit, right? So I think we need to be cautious.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I think Mike mentioned it. There is geopolitics, there is the macro that we are watching.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: On the other hand, there is secular growth in our market. So in the long term, I'm still very optimistic, we want to play quarter by quarter. That's part of the way we have guided 5.2 billion dollars in the midpoint.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Let's let TUCU play out and we'll call it as we see it. I remind you that the way we support our customers, the way we go to market, we go, we serve our customers direct.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: We have very friendly customer terms, so we see the build up of demand as we go almost real time.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: And I want to see, let's let TUCU play out and see if this growth is sustainable. That's the biggest question I have for myself for the second half.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: But at least the fact that industrial is still trending below previous peaks and the secular growth in data center and of course the content growth in automotive makes me feel optimistic about the long term.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Rafael, can you comment about loading?
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I just said that we have the capacity and the inventory that were well positioned on both of those to handle a wide range of scenarios in this up term.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: All right, today. Thank you so much for the questions. I'll move on to our next caller.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Our next question is from Joe Moore with Morgan family.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Great, thank you. Yeah, on the topic of fab loading, can you talk about what's going to happen with inventory over the course of Q2?
speaker: JOSEPH MOORE - MORGAN STANLEY

text: And are you seeing incremental gross margins off of Q1 that are sort of better than normal? Where's the normal, just normal?
speaker: JOSEPH MOORE - MORGAN STANLEY

text: What are the dynamics around that transition?
speaker: JOSEPH MOORE - MORGAN STANLEY

text: Yeah, again, we're well positioned and inventory objective of inventories to maintain high levels of customer service, keep lead times short and stable.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: And we're accomplishing that. So we feel very good as to where those are and will continue to determine what makes sense from a loadings and inventory standpoint throughout the quarter to handle any range of scenarios.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: And Joe, just to add on that, you and I talked a month ago, we saw a rapid growth in Q1 and inventory served as well, right?
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: We've depleted some of it. We've filled our customers real time according to their demand.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: And we just want to see how sustainable that would be. But as Rafael said, if it is in the market, once we have a very rapid growth and maybe catch up to trendline even quicker, we are well positioned.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Of course, we are in this phase three on the fabs and we can modulate more starts there. We have the capacity.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: We may make some incremental investments on the 80s because we are seeing on the assembly and test side a little bit of a tighter environment, at least externally.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: So as you know, we've brought most of our supply internally and we have that knob as well.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: We are very excited about the fact that we are prepared.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: If the market wants to grow at the same rate of Q1, we mentioned 19% over here. We are ready. If it wants to accelerate, we are ready as well.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: All right.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Jody, you follow up?
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Jody, you have a follow up?
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Well, just on the question is also on the gross margin aspect of that.
speaker: JOSEPH MOORE - MORGAN STANLEY

text: Do you, if the incremental gross margin is going to look normal or is there some part of inventory management that makes it less or more?
speaker: JOSEPH MOORE - MORGAN STANLEY

text: Yeah, no, the fault through that you should expect is in the 75 to 85% that we have guided that's excluding depreciation over a long term.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: But on a year and year basis, you look at our midpoint on EPS and revenue and make the right assumptions on topics and other lines, you should get to a reasonable assumption on gross margins.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: And it will be in that fault that we have guided.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Thank you.
speaker: JOSEPH MOORE - MORGAN STANLEY

text: Thanks for this question.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Moving on to our next caller.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Our next question is from Stacey Radskin with Bernstein Research.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Hi guys. Thanks for taking my question.
speaker: STACY RASGON - BERNSTEIN

text: Maybe just to dig into that gross margin point, if I sort of, I might typically think of your off XOPWAT a couple of points in Q2.
speaker: STACY RASGON - BERNSTEIN

text: I'd come up with a gross margin implicit in the guidance, maybe low to mid 59% up from 58.
speaker: STACY RASGON - BERNSTEIN

text: And it's up, I don't know, 100 or 150 bits you have a year on a pretty material revenue growth.
speaker: STACY RASGON - BERNSTEIN

text: Like part of it would almost expect it.
speaker: STACY RASGON - BERNSTEIN

text: The incremental gross margin to be higher given the revenue.
speaker: STACY RASGON - BERNSTEIN

text: But maybe it's differential just like the increase in depreciation or like, how should I be thinking about the different drivers of gross margin in DQ2?
speaker: STACY RASGON - BERNSTEIN

text: Quantitatively, if you don't want to give us a quantitative.
speaker: STACY RASGON - BERNSTEIN

text: Yeah, so Stacey, to help you out a little bit, your off X assumption was not a bad one.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: So you should expect some growth in off X first or second.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Maybe what you're missing is the acquisition charges line.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: You should expect to continue to have charges there every quarter at the tune of what we just reported in first quarter.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: We should continue, we'll continue having those there every quarter until we close, at which time there'll be a lot higher.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: And then there'll be steady after that for a number of years.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: But for now, for second quarter, just assume somewhere in the range of what we just reported on the acquisition line.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: When you do that, you'll get a gross margin assumption that should make sense.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: All right, give a follow-up.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: I do, thanks.
speaker: STACY RASGON - BERNSTEIN

text: Maybe to ask about the acquisition itself, not the deals, but I know you've talked about it being a creative.
speaker: STACY RASGON - BERNSTEIN

text: You guys are like one of the few, if not maybe the only company in my coverage, certainly that still does a pure gap earnings.
speaker: STACY RASGON - BERNSTEIN

text: And I even remember when you bought Natsemi, you did pro forma for a little while and then kind of said this is stupid.
speaker: STACY RASGON - BERNSTEIN

text: We're going back to gap.
speaker: STACY RASGON - BERNSTEIN

text: You guys make whatever adjustments you want to make.
speaker: STACY RASGON - BERNSTEIN

text: So, do your intentions for how you're going to report once you do close a slab?
speaker: STACY RASGON - BERNSTEIN

text: Because I have a hard time getting it a creative on a gap basis.
speaker: STACY RASGON - BERNSTEIN

text: Are you going to be going to a pro forma or how should we be thinking about that?
speaker: STACY RASGON - BERNSTEIN

text: You know, I think in right now is we will do gap, but we'll give you all the pieces that you need to do your own non-gap in whichever way you want to do that.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: So, we'll have the acquisition charges line, for example.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: You can take that out if you like and not count it.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: So, once we're on a run rate basis, all those will be non-cash.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: But initially, some of those are cash charges, right?
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: They're charges to cost to the bankers, the lawyers, the regulatory fees, et cetera.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: There'll be other things like the first quarter will have some weird transitions in gross margins and inventory as we write up the inventory that we're buying.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: So, we'll give you all those pieces that way you can do the non-gap analysis yourself.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Thanks for the question.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Thank you.
speaker: STACY RASGON - BERNSTEIN

text: Moving on to our next caller, please.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Our next question is from Ross Seymour with Deutsche Bank.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Hi, guys. Thanks so many ask a couple questions.
speaker: ROSS SEYMORE - DEUTSCHE BANK

text: I guess the first one is, given the strength that you saw, I guess, what was the biggest surprise versus the midpoint of your guide in the first quarter and was pricing part of the strength in either the quarter of the guide?
speaker: ROSS SEYMORE - DEUTSCHE BANK

text: Yeah, let me start maybe with pricing and then we can chat a little bit more about what happened in the quarter.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I think we answered it, but I'll repeat the same message as D.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: It's not surprising.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I think we said in the last quarter, we don't expect pricing to help the growth, at least not sequential all year over year.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: And that was the case, but it was better than our model.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Usually Q1 pricing is a couple of points down, call it the low single digits down year over year, and also sequentially because usually the price agreement, they key keen in the beginning of the year.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: So the quarter behaved a little better.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: We had pricing was the stable flat.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: If you will like for like both sequentially Q4 to Q1 and also year on year 1, Q26 versus 1, Q25.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: So that helped a little bit.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: And I expect Q2 to be very similar Ross.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Just the way we work with our customers, these are discussions that are not happening immediately.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: We serve them direct.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: And I will mention that as I look at the year, if demand, and right now the demand signals are strong, if demand continues to be strong, and we are monitoring the market price, and there is definitely at least an average price increase in the last several months across the annual market.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I think it's likely that prices may go up in the second half of the year.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Again, this is going to be a case by case discussion in our case, but that's a pricing environment as I see it right now.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: And again, it's always a function of supply and demand.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: And the unknown for me right now is the sustainability of demand.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: So I want to see it playing out one more quarter, and then we'll figure out for the second half.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: So high level, not immediate support on growth, both sequentially year over year on pricing.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Now what we have seen is just breadth of demand, right?
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: What I said before, multiple sectors, all sectors, all regions, all types of customers, small, large, and supported by a data center market where we do pretty well.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I think our portfolio is growing.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I believe we are fulfilling customer demand at the highest level.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: We have no shortages, and it allows us, I believe, to over time at least take market show over there.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: So that's, I think, what drove Q1.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I expect a similar behavior in Q2, and the second half of the year is still unknown.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: We are seeing, as I mentioned before, a higher tension on the analog side.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I think we see strength over there, and I think we are unique in the setup, in the sense that we have the capacity, we have the inventory, and we are well positioned to support customers at the highest level.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: You have a follow-up, Ross?
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Yeah, I do.
speaker: ROSS SEYMORE - DEUTSCHE BANK

text: One of the concerns people have, and it doesn't sound like in the strong report and guide that you guys are seeing it, but one of the concerns people had was more consumer oriented and marketing demand destruction with higher memory costs, memory availability, those sorts of things.
speaker: ROSS SEYMORE - DEUTSCHE BANK

text: Are you seeing any evidence of that?
speaker: ROSS SEYMORE - DEUTSCHE BANK

text: Your personal electronic segment seemed like it was well better than normal seasonal in the first quarter.
speaker: ROSS SEYMORE - DEUTSCHE BANK

text: I suspect that's where it would arise if it were to arise, and so I just wondered if you guys have seen any evidence of that across your business.
speaker: ROSS SEYMORE - DEUTSCHE BANK

text: High level, we have not.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Although customers are very aware of it, but I think they are doing well preparing themselves, and I'll let my comment about the personal electronics market.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I think it's also important to remember that fourth quarter last year was pretty easy to compare for the sequential transition for PE.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: On a year on year, it's about flat, so again, if that was happening, I don't know if you could point to those results as evidence of that, but again, you can't rule that out.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Actually, I think we are moving on to our next caller.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Thank you, Ross.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Thanks, Ross.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Now, our next question is from Tori Sondberg with DFL.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Yes, thank you, and congrats on the strong results.
speaker: TORE SVANBERG - STIFEL

text: I believe it was something to zoom in on on data center and specifically power.
speaker: TORE SVANBERG - STIFEL

text: It's a great market, great opportunity.
speaker: TORE SVANBERG - STIFEL

text: It's also very competitive.
speaker: TORE SVANBERG - STIFEL

text: And I'm just wondering if you could talk a little bit more about some of the modes here as we go into the next few years that TI has.
speaker: TORE SVANBERG - STIFEL

text: I do assume your manufacturing footprint will be an important element of that, but any other caller you could add on TI's positioning in power semis, especially with data center next next few years.
speaker: TORE SVANBERG - STIFEL

text: Yeah, Tori, I think, look, power in general is very, very important to data centers, as we know, and specifically power density.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: And we talk about both the, just think about the amount of power or the energy you have to drive into these systems.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: You need a lot of silicon to withstand it, right?
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: So that implies on the importance of power electronics and TI's well positioned.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: What I like about our position is this combination.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: And that's, by the way, is true for every market, but in data center, I think there is a lot of attention to the data center.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: And I think that's what I call application specific sockets.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: You can call it stage one, stage two, the VRN, the last, you know, the V-Core that gives GPUs, you know, they need the power delivery at the highest level, very complex parts, multi-phase power delivery, et cetera.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: And there is also a lot, a lot of general purpose parts in Iraq.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I would say tens of thousands of them, lots of different skills, and this is where our general purpose portfolio is amazing.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: We can fulfill, I would say, almost every analog socket on these racks.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: And I think we are very unique in that point, not only because of the breadth of the portfolio, but also because our ability to supply.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: And I think we have seen cases where our customers need help because they had supply, you know, shortages from their other suppliers, and we come in and solve the problem.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I think that's part of the reason our growth has been so high.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I mentioned 90% over the year, and I'm very excited about the future there.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: So that combination of a broad portfolio and the ability to support customers with, you know, capacity and inventory is unique.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: The second point, which I think I've touched upon in many calls or conferences, we are also investing more and more R&D in data center,
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: and we are going to be one of the competitors on the application-specific sockets, whether it's VRN in stage two,
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: whether it's high voltage, you know, 800 to 12 or 6 at stage one, and we are well positioned there as well, both with the GAN technologies that we've invested in in the past 15 years,
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: but also now very advanced BCD nodes that not only has the capacity needed, but also it's built in North America here in Texas,
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: and customers care a lot about it.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: So I think that combination of broad portfolio, both on general purpose and ASSPs, the ability to support the rec, not only the board,
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: and ability to supply at scale with the tonnage, if you will, or the volume that this market demand is very, very unique,
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: not to mention that it's come from a geopolitically dependable location.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: So all of that is the unique combination, and that's part of what we like to talk about our competitive advantages.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Maybe one of them is easy to replicate, but trying to replicate all, in this case all three is not easy,
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: and this is why I'm very encouraged about our opportunity to continue to grow in this market.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I will just add that our application-specific sockets are seeing momentum as well on the design interface right now,
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: and I do expect that they'll kick in more in the second half of the year and into 2027,
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: so my bar for the team and my expectations are high here.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Thanks, Joy. Do you have a follow-up?
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Yeah, that's great, Paul. Thank you, Harvey, for that. And then there's my follow-up.
speaker: TORE SVANBERG - STIFEL

text: Just thinking about, obviously now we're in another new up cycle in analog,
speaker: TORE SVANBERG - STIFEL

text: and just comparing this to the last one, I mean the last one capacity got tight pretty quickly,
speaker: TORE SVANBERG - STIFEL

text: and Lee started extending pretty quickly. I know it's a different cycle, right?
speaker: TORE SVANBERG - STIFEL

text: But I'm just curious now that you've made all the capex investments, you've got the big manufacturing footprint.
speaker: TORE SVANBERG - STIFEL

text: Are you starting to see share gains sort of pop up in your design wins,
speaker: TORE SVANBERG - STIFEL

text: since you are in much better position with capacity and now we're suspecting?
speaker: TORE SVANBERG - STIFEL

text: I believe we are, yes, and we have gained share, of course, in analog in 2025,
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: but I think we have a lot of room to go. We are still below previous peaks,
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: and to me the question, Tori, can we do it quickly,
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: meaning does the demand, or does the strong demand continue, or it's going to take us more time?
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: From our perspective, we hope the demand continues. We have the answer to customers,
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: and in many cases we are unique. I gave a minute ago the data center example,
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: but we are starting to see other areas where our supply, our availability,
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: is allowing us to win back market share.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: So I mentioned pricing before, our pricing is very competitive,
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I think we have an opportunity there as well for the second half of the year.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: So it all depends on the sustainability of the demand. I think Vivek mentioned before,
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: we had a very unique 2025, but it started strong and then it took a breather.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I want to see it playing out in 2026. Obviously, if it continues, our opportunity just grows.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: May I just add that we spent the last several years preparing with capacity and inventories you know,
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: and our lead times have been stable over the last several years,
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: and especially last several months. Really happy with the delivery performance.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: And so as we look at what the future holds here,
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: we want to make sure we can service our customers needs, also their growth as well,
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: across a broad customer base, and we're really happy with the systems we have in place to allow that.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: All right, move on to our next caller.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Our next question is from Matthew Pritzka with Cantor Fitzgerald.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Hey guys, thanks for taking the question.
speaker: MATTHEW PRISCO - CANTOR FITZGERALD

text: So you previously talked about spending about two to three billion CAPEX in 2026.
speaker: MATTHEW PRISCO - CANTOR FITZGERALD

text: First, is that still the right number? And then as we think about the modular buildouts within its ongoing recovery,
speaker: MATTHEW PRISCO - CANTOR FITZGERALD

text: can you maybe help walk us through when you would need to start to add the incremental equipment
speaker: MATTHEW PRISCO - CANTOR FITZGERALD

text: and how you're thinking of strategically about your capacity today as we start to see some boundary capacity,
speaker: MATTHEW PRISCO - CANTOR FITZGERALD

text: because the mature nodes are now two to two boundary pricing increases?
speaker: MATTHEW PRISCO - CANTOR FITZGERALD

text: Yes, I'll start. First, the answer to your question is yes.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: We're looking at two to three billion dollars of CAPEX for this year.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: And in that number, there's capacity for what we call phase three, which is incremental capacity that may you're alluding to.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: So that's both for the in the fab side, but also in the assembly test assembly test side.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: And that is where a growing proportion of our CAPEX is going to in the assembly test side to address growth.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Beyond that, what I would tell you for CAPEX beyond 26 is think of the 1.2 times rate that we have talked about before.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: For the long term needs of the long term CAPEX intensity.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: So for example, if you to make a number easy 5% growth would translate into 6% CAPEX as percent of revenue.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: And that's how you would want to model it.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Matthew, just one more point. I think Rafael touched upon it. So again, two to three very valid.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: But remember, we gave a framework that I still very valid.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I think it was a couple of years back during capital management on kind of revenue scenarios and CAPEX.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I think these are also very, very valid. I will say that right, Rafael alluded to, we are seeing right now,
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: even at the midpoint of the second quarter. And again, I want to see how it plays out.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: But we're looking at this, I don't know, 17, 18% growth year for the first half of the year.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: That's stronger than last year. So of course, we want to be prepared in case it continues.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: No one tells us what the future will be. We just have to support a range of scenarios.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: So in that sense, we are taking the opportunity to divert some of the,
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: because we have enough that the thing about wafer capacity, I think we are well positioned without 300 millimeter wafer fabs.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: We have the brick and mortar. We have the installed equipment. But on the 80th side, I think there is an opportunity.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: And we are very happy that we've internalized our supply because we are seeing more and more button necks in the market.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: They're popping up. And the fact that we control our destiny here and we can move more stuff internally is a benefit.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: So some of these 2.3 billion dollar of cap bags that you are seeing this year, some of that is going to support a faster internalization of our back end into our own assembly and test.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: And that allows us to support customers at a higher level.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Give a follow-up, Matt.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Yeah, it's awful. Thanks. I guess the follow-up is there any update to your messaging around depreciation expectations first three months ago?
speaker: MATTHEW PRISCO - CANTOR FITZGERALD

text: And then maybe how to think about timing of when CI will receive the remaining CHIPSAC direct funding?
speaker: MATTHEW PRISCO - CANTOR FITZGERALD

text: Thanks.
speaker: MATTHEW PRISCO - CANTOR FITZGERALD

text: Yeah, so I'll take that. Not change to the expectations for this year, 2.2 to 2.4.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: And then for 2027, continue upward pressure, but likely at a slower rate.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: On the CHIPSAC, first I will tell you the more interesting one is ITC. We've been talking about that one.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: That's the one that's going to give us more money for the long term.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: And that's a 35% of qualified manufacturing investments.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: We have been getting that ITC and then we'll continue to get ITC.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: But on the direct funding, we just received over $500 million.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: In total, what we received in fourth quarter is $630 million out of the up to $1.6 billion of direct funding.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: And the remaining, we should get that over the coming years as we continue fulfilling the various milestones stipulated in the contract.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Thanks, Matt. Move on to our next card, please.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Now, our next question is from Joe Klutroce with Wells Fargo.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Yeah, thanks for taking the question.
speaker: JOSEPH QUATROCHI - WELLS FARGO

text: I was curious if you could maybe just help us understand, you know, given the resegmentation of revenue, especially on the industrial side, you know, what is normal seasonality now for the June quarter?
speaker: JOSEPH QUATROCHI - WELLS FARGO

text: So if you could probably look back and model out what our revenue is done over history and I don't have a buy-in market, you know, specific, you know, what the percentage is.
speaker: JOSEPH QUATROCHI - WELLS FARGO

text: But overall, what you'll typically see is the second and third quarter or stronger quarters and fourth and first are typically lower compared to second and third.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: You have a follow-up?
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: I'll just add on that, but just in seasonality, look, our guide is, I would describe it as, you know, a little bit above seasonality, right?
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I think we have guided that, what, 8% sequential?
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: So that's a little bit.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: And again, the combination of the market is changing.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Data center, as we know, is now a bigger part of our revenue.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: But overall, my view on 2Q is it's a slightly above seasonal guide.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Hopefully that helps.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Jody, you have a follow-up?
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Yeah, the follow-up.
speaker: JOSEPH QUATROCHI - WELLS FARGO

text: You know, a really strong quarter and the first quarter of the gate for free cash flow and just even cash flow from operations.
speaker: JOSEPH QUATROCHI - WELLS FARGO

text: Is there any update on just how to think about free cash flow per share for this year?
speaker: JOSEPH QUATROCHI - WELLS FARGO

text: Any change there?
speaker: JOSEPH QUATROCHI - WELLS FARGO

text: Yeah, I think I mentioned, Joe, during the capital management call that as long as revenue is growing, you know, mid to high single digits, that $8 free cash flow per share is very probably, highly profitable.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Okay.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Now, as I said before, you know, first half of the year at the midpoint is somewhere between 15 and 20% growth, right?
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: So there's definitely an upside.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: I'm not going to say what the number is, but go back to our framework that we provided back in the capital management call.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: You'll see, I think at 20 billion, we had an 8 to 9 and at 22, we had a 9 to 10.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: So it gives you kind of how every extra billion dollars of revenue is doing, what are we held free cash flow per share?
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: It gives you a very high level of framework.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: But right now, assuming we don't have another full start, I think there is a very high, very likely we will be that will easily be that $8 free cash flow per share for 2026.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Again, we need to see how the yield continues, but I would say the probability is probably high.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Thank you, Joe. Move on to our last caller.
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Our last question is from Chris Kaysa with Wolf Research.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Yes, thank you. First question will be about fab loadings.
speaker: CHRISTOPHER CASO - WOLFE RESEARCH

text: And given what appears to be a strong start to the year, what are your plans for fab loadings?
speaker: CHRISTOPHER CASO - WOLFE RESEARCH

text: And what do you expect to do with inventory as we go through the year?
speaker: CHRISTOPHER CASO - WOLFE RESEARCH

text: I know you've been building inventory in order to be responsive to customers.
speaker: CHRISTOPHER CASO - WOLFE RESEARCH

text: You expect to keep inventory of these levels, or might that dip a bit?
speaker: CHRISTOPHER CASO - WOLFE RESEARCH

text: Yes, so we feel very comfortable with our position with both capacity and inventory.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: And inventory is there to support customer satisfaction, keep leads on short and stable.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: So we'll continue to do that and we'll adjust loadings throughout the quarter to handle whatever comes at us in a number of scenarios in this upturn.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Yes, we had on that, Chris, we talked about all these phases of our investment, phase one, phase two, phase three.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: So right now the surge of demand is in analog, right?
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: And in analog, we are at phase three. So we are modulating start. We have the capacity.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: We are modulating start real time. We are just looking at the daily consumption, if you will.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: And this is very rough. I'll guide the team of to start way first.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: So of course, we have the opportunity. Now, in terms of inventory, it all depends on the rate of consumption.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Meaning if demand continues to be very, very strong, we'll continue to defeat inventory.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Obviously, it takes time to build this part, right? Some of the parts get built in three months, but some of them can take six to nine months.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: So overall, that's why we have inventory. Inventory allows us a quick surge of customer support.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: If they have a strong demand, that's what happened in Q1.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: We have a strong guide for Q2 in the midpoint, it's 8% sequential. It's above season, as I mentioned.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: So I think inventory will play a role there. But then the machine catches up.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: So to me, all these questions are related to what the second half of the year of demand will do.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: And based on the macro environment and based on what happened last year in terms of the market's logitary, as I called it before,
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I want to see it play out. The good news is that we are prepared for every scenario that will be presented to us.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Chris, you're up. Yeah, let me just add a taking a longer picture and just the next quarter, when you think of our range of inventory days, 150 to 250,
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: during an upturn, we should be draining that number.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Right now we're at 209, it should shift drift towards the lower end.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: And then during the downturn, that's when we build inventory and then it moves upward, right?
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: So at a new level, in an ideal scenario, that's what you would see in terms of days of inventory.
speaker: RAFAEL LIZARDI - SENIOR VP & CFO

text: Chris, do you have a follow-up?
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: I do. And for my follow-up, I want to return to some of your comments about pricing.
speaker: CHRISTOPHER CASO - WOLFE RESEARCH

text: And we've heard from some others in the space who were a little more explicit on what they were doing with pricing.
speaker: CHRISTOPHER CASO - WOLFE RESEARCH

text: Is TI simply following the market right now on what's happened with your comments of potentially some better pricing in the second half?
speaker: CHRISTOPHER CASO - WOLFE RESEARCH

text: And then as a follow-on to that, to what extent are your customers, what percentage of your customers would be on sort of annual price contracts such that if there was a reset in pricing,
speaker: CHRISTOPHER CASO - WOLFE RESEARCH

text: that would more likely happen toward the end of the year and to next year?
speaker: CHRISTOPHER CASO - WOLFE RESEARCH

text: Yeah, I think Chris, I think it's a good question and I think we touched most of it.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Just to clarify, TI follows yes because we want to see sustainability, right?
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: We don't want to be changing prices every quarter.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Of course prices go up and down every quarter, it depends on their portfolio and where customers need more demand and more supplies, et cetera.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Let's look at 2025. In 2025, our pricing behaved as we expected.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: It was down this low single-digit number. That was the actual number in 2025.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: In the first quarter of 2016, it was stable. It was a good start of the year.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: I think if demand continues to behave like that, and we see stronger and stronger requests from our customers, that opens up a discussion.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: And that's what we are going through right now.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: We are definitely seeing, if you go back to the supply agreement or price agreement we have done, they were agreed upon.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Last year, somewhere in Q4. The demand environment was very different then.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: We are seeing higher numbers in terms of demand.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: We will have to invest in our capacity. I mentioned back-end capacity investment to support all of that.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: There is a tightness on the offset world. So of course, it's a discussion.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: And I think customers are very thoughtful and most important for them is not to have a 30 cents part stopping their production.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: They need to have high level of customer support. That's what we are offering.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Not only in supporting the part we promised them, but also sometimes solving problems they have with other suppliers.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: That's the opportunity we have in 2026. But again, all depends on the sustainability of the demand signal.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: We will continue to watch it. We are discussing with our customers as we speak.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: And we will report back during the July call.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Thanks, Chris. Have you want to close us out?
speaker: MIKE BECKMAN - VP & HEAD OF INVESTOR RELATIONS

text: Yes. So let me wrap up with what we've said previously. At our core, we are engineers and technology is a foundation of our company.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: But ultimately our objective and the best metric to measure progress and generate value to owners is the long-term growth of free customer share.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: Thank you all and have a good evening.
speaker: HAVIV ILAN - PRESIDENT, CEO & CHAIRMAN

text: This concludes today's conference. You may disconnect your lines at this time.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thank you again for your participation.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thank you.
speaker: OPERATOR
